title: Bolivihanburya paranganiensis
taxonomy: Bolivihanburya paranganiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to Bolivia and was first described in 2004. As a member of the Annonaceae family, Bolivihanburya paranganiensis is closely related to other flowering plants in this family, which are commonly known as custard apples. The exact classification of Bolivihanburya paranganiensis within the Annonaceae family is not well-documented. Further research is needed to determine its specific classification.
constituents: Bolivihanburya paranganiensis is a plant species that, although not widely documented, likely contains various bioactive compounds.  Its active constituents are expected to include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, similar to those found in other plants within the family Annonaceae. The exact composition of Bolivihanburya paranganiensis may also include terpenoids, glycosides, and saponins, contributing to its potential medicinal properties. However, the specific profile of active constituents in Bolivihanburya paranganiensis has not been extensively studied or documented. Further research is necessary to understand the full range of bioactive compounds present in this plant species.
